title: Changing the Game for Seniors
subtitle: 一位医生25年的坚持：如何重塑老年照护方式

paragraphs.en: Even though we are living in an age where growing old is thought of as an inevitable misery, this doctor has been changing the game for seniors over the last 25 years. It all started in 1991 when the Harvard-educated physician was transferred from working in a stressful emergency room to being the medical director of a nursing home in upstate New York. The depressing and regimented environment got him thinking on what exactly could improve the residents' conditions.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管我们生活在一个视衰老为不可避免的苦难的时代，但这位医生在过去25年里彻底改变了老年人的生活方式。一切始于1991年，这位哈佛毕业的医生从紧张的急诊室调任纽约州北部一家疗养院的医疗主任。压抑而死板的环境让他开始思考究竟怎样才能改善住户的状况。

words.meaning: adj. 严格管制的；死板的

paragraphs.en: Even though animals in nursing homes were illegal at the time, Dr. Bill Thomas took a chance. Based on a hunch, he brought in two dogs, four cats, hens, rabbits, 100 birds, a multitude of plants, a flower garden, and a vegetable patch. The change was dramatic. There was a 50% drop in medical prescriptions along with a dramatic decrease in death rates — but most importantly, the residents were simply happier.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管当时在疗养院养动物是违规的，比尔·托马斯博士还是决定放手一搏。凭着直觉，他引进了两只狗、四只猫、母鸡、兔子、100只鸟、大量植物、一个花园和一块菜地。变化是巨大的：医疗处方减少了50%，死亡率也大幅下降——但最重要的是，住户们明显更快乐了。

paragraphs.en: Dr. Thomas's approach, named the Eden Alternative, has driven nursing homes to allow a more autonomous and creative living space for their elderly. It erases the belief that growing old means growing useless. He encourages residents to think of their age as an enriching new phase of life rather than the end of it.
paragraphs.zh: 托马斯博士的这套方法被命名为「伊甸园替代方案」，推动疗养院为老年人提供更自主、更有创造力的生活空间。它打破了变老就意味着变无用的观念。他鼓励住户将自己的年龄视为人生丰富的新阶段，而非终点。

paragraphs.en: Thomas, now a speaker and author of several books, also created small, independently-run residences with their own bedrooms and bathrooms, and he has been preaching a singular message that getting old is not a bad thing. "Within six weeks, they had to send a truck around to pick up all the wheelchairs," Thomas told the Washington Post. "You know why most people in nursing homes use wheelchairs? Because the buildings are so big."
paragraphs.zh: 如今已是演讲家和多本书作者的托马斯，还创建了拥有独立卧室和浴室的小型自管住宅，并一直传递一个核心信息：变老并不是坏事。托马斯告诉《华盛顿邮报》：「六周内，他们就得派卡车来收走所有的轮椅。你知道疗养院里大多数人为什么坐轮椅吗？因为楼太大了。」

paragraphs.en: The 56-year-old doctor's methods have been adopted in Australia, Japan, Canada, and America with enormous success. Last year he published Second Wind: Navigating the Passage to a Slower, Deeper, and More Connected Life, a guide on how to shift our perspectives on aging and growth. He is currently traveling through North America performing with his guitar and his enthusiasm on his Age of Disruption Tour.
paragraphs.zh: 这位56岁医生的方法已在澳大利亚、日本、加拿大和美国广泛推行，并取得了巨大成功。去年他出版了《第二春》，一本关于如何转变我们对衰老和成长看法的指南。他目前正带着吉他和满腔热情，在北美进行「颠覆年龄」巡回演出。
